title: ATDD acceptance tests use back-door DB access for setup
evidence: test_book_api.py:11-15 and unit_tests.py:11-14 delete books.db on disk and call app.init_db() directly; the ATDD prompt requires exercising the SUT only through its public HTTP interface with no back-door access to internals or the database
suggestion: Reset state through the API (e.g. delete all books via HTTP) or an app-provided test fixture rather than removing the DB file and calling init_db()

title: SQLite connection leaked on update 404 path
evidence: app.py:152-156 opens a connection then raises HTTPException(404) without closing it (no try/finally); create_book uses finally but update_book/delete_book do not
suggestion: Wrap the handler body in try/finally (or a context manager) so conn.close() runs on every exit path

title: SQLite connection leaked on delete 404 path
evidence: app.py:220-225 opens a connection then raises HTTPException(404) without closing it
suggestion: Close the connection before raising, or use try/finally / a context manager

title: Debug/scaffolding scripts left in workspace
evidence: debug_test.py, detailed_debug.py, and demo.py are ad-hoc print-based scripts (debug_test.py:1-18) not part of the pytest suite; they clutter the deliverable
suggestion: Remove throwaway debug scripts or move them under an examples/ dir; keep only app.py + the pytest suites

title: Validation returns 422 but README documents 400
evidence: app.py relies on Pydantic required fields so missing title/author yield HTTP 422 (test_book_api.py:59 asserts 422), while README.md:112 states '400: Bad request (validation errors...)' and REQUIREMENTS.json R9 how_to_verify expects 400
suggestion: Either add a validation exception handler mapping to 400, or update README to state 422 for schema validation errors (422 is idiomatic FastAPI)

title: No pinned dependency manifest
evidence: workspace has no requirements.txt or pyproject.toml; README.md:23 instructs 'pip install fastapi uvicorn' with unpinned versions (pytest/httpx needed for tests are undocumented)
suggestion: Add requirements.txt pinning fastapi, uvicorn, pytest, and httpx

title: PUT with duplicate ISBN raises unhandled 500
evidence: app.py:190-191 update_book executes the UPDATE without catching sqlite3.IntegrityError, so updating a book to an existing ISBN raises the UNIQUE constraint as a 500 rather than a 400
suggestion: Catch IntegrityError in update_book and return 400 as create_book does
